_comment: WinDroid compatibility database (seed). Data, not code: add an app by adding an entry here. 'match' identifies the app (exe filename, optional size/sha256, name substring, or steam app id); 'profile' is the known-good configuration. Empty/omitted profile fields inherit the container default. Ratings: playable | runnable | ingame | blocked-anticheat | broken | unknown.

apps._note: Migrated from Win32AppWorkarounds: esync breaks these titles.

Disable esync.

Fixed-resolution fix.

apps._note: --- UWP / Microsoft Store apps: not runnable under Wine (no MSIX/Store/WinRT stack) ---

profile.notes: The Xbox app for Windows is a UWP/MSIX Microsoft Store app; it needs the Windows Store deployment stack, Gaming Services and WinRT, which Wine does not implement. Not runnable. Install games directly (their own launchers/exes) instead.
Xbox Console Companion needs Xbox Live + WinRT; not runnable under Wine.
Microsoft Gaming Services is a UWP/system component; not runnable under Wine.
The Microsoft Store is a pure UWP/WinRT app tied to Windows' AppX deployment service and licensing; Wine has no UWP runtime, so it cannot run. Download apps' standalone installers instead.
AppX Deployment Service — a Windows UWP subsystem, not present under Wine.

apps._note: --- FRONTIER-A Tier-0: GDI/USER single-exes, zero graphics config (merry-moth staged, eager-wren reviewed) ---

profile.notes: Pure Win32/GDI PDF reader; no 3D. WineHQ Platinum. WinDroid on-device unverified (pending arm64 HW).
GDI + winsock SSH client. WineHQ Platinum. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

apps._note: --- FRONTIER-A Tier-1: .NET Framework -> wine-mono 10.1.0 proof ---

profile.notes: .NET 4.x WinForms + crypto - the best wine-mono smoke (no DX). WineHQ Gold with Mono. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

apps._note: sha256-PINNED exact build: ImageGlass 8.12.4.30 x64 (last .NET-Framework 8.x line). keen-wren oracle: CLR-header present = .NET Framework 4.x -> wine-mono runs it; 0 real DX imports (dxvk-default inert). Precise row wins (SCORE_SHA256=5) over the name-match fallback below.

profile.notes: ImageGlass 8.12.4.30 x64 (github.com/d2phap/ImageGlass, ImageGlass_8.12.4.30_x64.zip) = the LAST .NET Framework 4.x release before v9 went modern .NET. Oracle-verified Framework (CLR header present, 'Mono/.Net assembly') -> wine-mono 10.1.0 runs it. 0 real DX imports (dxvk default inert). runnable per WineHQ mono consensus; on-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

BROAD name-match FALLBACK — intentionally FLAVOR-NEUTRAL (no mono, no dxwrapper) per swift-wolf-fb .NET rule: runtime flavor is a per-VERSION fact carried by the sha256 pins, not a per-NAME fact. ImageGlass <=8.x = .NET Framework (mono-runnable, pinned above @8.12.4.30); >=9.x = modern .NET (needs Desktop Runtime, unsupported → its own future sha256 pin). Asserting mono here would be a false-GREEN for 9.x users. Non-pinned 8.x builds still get mono via Wine's AddonsURL auto-install (Framework detected at runtime), so neutral fallback loses nothing. Rated 'unknown' since name-match can't know the version.

apps._note: --- FRONTIER-A Tier-2: DirectX -> DXVK 2.4.1 / VKD3D / wincomponents ---

profile.notes: D3D11 (DXVK) GBA emulator; clean small swap-chain test. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending; DXVK needs working Vulkan - 8 Elite/Turnip ideal, AVD Vulkan is the vortek gate).
OpenGL/DirectDraw; low-spec, self-contained. WineD3D safe (no Vulkan dep). Good sustained-render test. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).
Legacy DirectDraw + directsound. directsound is default-on (DEFAULT_WINCOMPONENTS), so no wincomponents override needed. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).
Heavier OpenGL/Vulkan 3D; validates the game-render path end-to-end (8 Elite + Turnip). On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

Minimal Dear ImGui D3D11 demo - isolates DXVK bring-up from game complexity. d3dcompiler_47 ships in the direct3d wincomponent (default-on), so no wincomponents override needed. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

profile.notes: Qt UI + media codecs. Only directshow needs toggling (default=0); wmdecoder + directsound are already default-on. Heavier; drive after Tier 0-2. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

apps._note: --- L1: witty-hawk L2 battery, PE-imports parsed + keen-wren oracle-verified (grounded, not guessed) ---

profile.notes: SDL2 source port; imports all wine-builtin, 0 real DX (SDL2/SDL2_mixer/net + libpng/zlib/fluidsynth bundled in-zip). Needs a DOOM WAD (data, not a DLL). dxvk default is inert (no D3D). On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

PSP emulator; static imports opengl32/dinput8/hid/winmm/ws2_32/winhttp — all builtin, 0 gaps. Defaults to Direct3D11 on Windows -> the container-default dxvk serves it (GL/Vulkan also selectable in-app; Vulkan backend needs Turnip on device). No dxwrapper override needed. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).
Diff/merge tool; 13 imports all wine-builtin (pure GDI + gdiplus), 0 gaps (VERIFY-B JUST-RUNS). No DX, no .NET. dxvk default inert. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).
Screenshot tool; imports mscoree.dll = .NET FRAMEWORK -> wine-mono 10.1.0 implements it (VERIFY-B JUST-RUNS*). Contrast screentogif (modern .NET, broken). On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

apps._note: ScreenToGif: sha256-PINNED exact build (keen-wren oracle, byte-identical to CompatEngine.sha256, VERIFY-A proven). This activates G1's SCORE_SHA256 tier in production (was data-inert). The pinned release is modern .NET framework-dependent → broken; a DIFFERENT (self-contained) build has a different hash → falls through to the name-match row below.

profile.notes: EXACT build (oracle-verified): MODERN .NET, FRAMEWORK-DEPENDENT single-file WPF (in-exe hostfxr/.NETCoreApp/PresentationCore, NO bundled coreclr) → needs the real .NET Desktop Runtime, which wine-mono does NOT provide → will not launch. NOT a .NET-Framework/mono case. Revisit if merry-crane's .NET-unlock lands a Desktop-Runtime provisioning path.

Name-match fallback (broad). ScreenToGif is modern .NET (Core/5+): a FRAMEWORK-DEPENDENT build needs the .NET Desktop Runtime (unsupported by wine-mono → broken); a SELF-CONTAINED build (bundles coreclr) would just-run (runnable). The verified fwdep-modern release is pinned by sha256 above (broken); other/unknown builds land here as 'unknown' rather than being flat-condemned. Do NOT set wineComponents=[mono] — modern .NET, mono won't help.

apps._note: --- L1 BATTERY-2: eager-wren VERIFY-B (keen-wren oracle-grounded imports, normal+delay) ---

profile.notes: voidtools Everything file-search; 10 imports all wine-builtin, pure Win32/GDI, 0 DX. The mscoree in the binary is a SUBSTRING false-pos — real import table has none, so NOT .NET (no mono). dxvk default inert (no D3D). On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

SNES emulator; oracle-confirmed REAL d3d9 + ddraw imports (dxgi is substring false-pos) → dxvk serves d3d9 (needs Vulkan on device; Adreno/S25 GPU=GO). First BATTERY-2 real-DX target. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).

lucasg Dependencies (PE-import viewer); imports mscoree = .NET FRAMEWORK → wine-mono is the right lever, BUT it's a WPF app and wine-mono's WPF coverage is incomplete → may fail to render. Rated unknown pending on-device (arm64 HW). Same managed-app-qualifier class as screentogif/imageglass.

apps._note: --- L1 FEEDER-BRIGHT batch-1: merry-moth-46 ran keen-wren pe_oracle.py first-hand on the staged exes (import tables below are REAL normal+delay imports, substring false-positives excluded). sha256 = lowercase hex, own PowerShell Get-FileHash, byte-matches bright-yak feed. ---

profile.notes: DuckStation PS1 emulator (x86_64). Oracle: REAL imports = msvcp140/vcruntime140/vcruntime140_1 ONLY (vcrun, all wine-builtin); the d3d11/d3d12/dxgi/mf/mfplat/xaudio2 strings are substring FALSE-POSITIVES, not imported (renders via its own runtime-selected backend, D3D device created dynamically). dxvk default stays inert until a D3D device is made — no dxwrapper override needed. Deps-justification: 0 import gaps (vcrun builtin). On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending); Vulkan backend needs Turnip/Adreno.

Godot 4.3 engine/editor (x86_64). Oracle: REAL imports = dinput8 + dxgi ONLY; d3d11/d3d12/xinput1_3/1_4/9_1_0 are substring FALSE-POSITIVES (not imported). NO dxwrapper set on purpose (quiet-yak Trap-4): Godot 4.x defaults to its Vulkan RenderingDevice backend which talks to the Vulkan ICD DIRECTLY, not through D3D → dxvk would be INERT here; the real dependency is a working Vulkan ICD (Turnip/Adreno on device), not a dxwrapper. GL-compatibility fallback renderer selectable in-editor. Rated unknown pending on-device (arm64 HW; Vulkan ICD is the gate). Deps-justification: dinput8+dxgi builtin; real renderer path is device-Vulkan-dependent, no wrapper helps.

apps._note: 32-bit x86 PE (oracle arch=x86, machine 0x14c). ARCH-GATE RESOLVED at build level (eager-wren t-f4fb5f / BOX64-32BIT-ARCH-GATE-VERDICT.md): box64-0.4.0 has BOX32 built-in (real symtab defs) + new-WoW64 wine → 32-bit x86 is supported, box86 NOT needed. Rated 'runnable' per lead policy (build-level YES); the ONE remaining owed item is the on-device confirmation (Greenshot Q6 canary), same device-pending hedge every runnable row carries.

profile.notes: HxD hex editor (32-bit x86, machine=0x14c). Oracle: 0 real DX/.NET/vcrun imports — pure Win32/GDI, just-runs on coverage. 32-bit-x86 execution is supported at build level (box64 BOX32 built-in + new-WoW64 wine, box86 not needed — eager-wren t-f4fb5f). Deps-justification: 0 import gaps, no arch blocker. On-device confirm pending (Greenshot Q6 is the class-wide 32-bit confirmation run; arm64 HW).

apps._note: bright-yak (row owner) flags an artifact split: qBittorrent's INSTALLER is 32-bit NSIS but the INSTALLED qbittorrent.exe is x86_64 on modern releases. This name-match row targets the installed app. 32-bit x86 is now build-supported anyway (box64 BOX32 built-in + new-WoW64 — eager-wren t-f4fb5f), so either arch runs; not arch-gated. NOTE: merry-moth did NOT independently measure this exe's machine word (not in staged test-targets) — arch claim is bright-yak's, not first-hand oracle.

profile.notes: qBittorrent (Qt Widgets UI, no D3D surface → dxvk default inert, no wrapper). Installed exe is x86_64 on modern builds (per bright-yak); 32-bit x86 is also build-supported (box64 BOX32 built-in + new-WoW64, box86 not needed — eager-wren t-f4fb5f), so no arch gate either way. Deps-justification: Qt/no import gaps flagged by gate. On-device unverified (arm64 HW pending).
